terms.definition: A graphical debugger embedded in an editor (VS Code, PyCharm) that controls a Python debug adapter through a protocol like DAP.
Microsoft's Python debug adapter; the engine underneath VS Code's Python debugger (and usable from any DAP-speaking client).
PyCharm's Python debugger engine; similar in role to debugpy but with a different protocol.
Microsoft-defined protocol that lets any editor talk to any language's debug adapter; the standard underneath modern IDE debuggers.
Editor file (`.vscode/launch.json` for VS Code; Run Configurations for PyCharm) that defines how to start or attach a debug session.
Debug session that connects to an already-running process listening on a debug port (`debugpy.listen`).
Debugger feature that emits a formatted log message when execution hits a line, without pausing — like a print without editing source.
Breakpoint that triggers only when a predicate evaluates true (`doc.id == 'doc-472'`).
Breakpoint set by *function name* rather than line; triggers wherever the function is defined.
Breakpoint that pauses when an exception of a chosen type is raised (whether caught or uncaught).
Attach-config setting that equates a local source path with a remote/container path so breakpoints match correctly.
